'''Dan Norman''' (born 8 September 1997) is a former [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland international]] [[rugby league]] footballer who last played {{rlp|pr}} for [[Leigh Leopards]] in the [[Super League]].

He previously played for the [[Widnes Vikings]] and [[St Helens RLFC|St Helens]] in the [[Super League]] and the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], and on loan from [[Widnes Vikings]] at the [[North Wales Crusaders]] in [[RFL League 1]]. He has also played for the [[London Broncos]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].<ref>{{Cite news|title=St Helens sign Broncos prop Norman|language=en-GB|work=BBC Sport|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55173566|access-date=2020-12-04}}</ref>

==Background==
{{BLP unsourced section|date=October 2024}}
He was born in [[Warrington]], [[Cheshire, England]].

Dan attended birchwood community high school.

==Career==
===Widnes Vikings===
Norman made his début for the [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] club in the Super League against [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] in June 2018. He played 20 games and only scored 1 try.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/44455884|title=Super League: Salford Red Devils 26-12 Widnes Vikings|work=BBC Sport |date=12 June 2018 }}</ref>

===North Wales Crusaders (loan)===
In 2018, Norman signed (on loan) for the [[North Wales Crusaders]] where he played 13 games and scored 1 try for them.{{citation needed|date=August 2023}}

===Salford Red Devils (loan)===
On 8 July 2021 it was reported that he had joined [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] in the [[Super League]] on a short loan<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=8 Jul 2021|accessdate=8 Jul 2021|title=Salford bring in duo on loan; Ellis Robson extends stay|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/salford-bring-in-duo-on-loan-ellis-robson-extends-stay/}}</ref>
On 9 August 2021 it was reported that he had re-joined the Salford club in the [[Super League]] on a one-week loan,<ref>{{cite web|website=St Helens Star|date=10 Aug 2021|accessdate=10 Aug 2021|title=Saints prop Dan Norman joins Salford Red Devils on loan|url=https://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/sport/19502964.saints-prop-dan-norman-joins-salford-red-devils-loan/}}</ref> following the announcement of {{rlp|PR}} [[Lee Mossop]]'s immediate retirement due to injury.

===Leigh Leopards (DR)===
He signed for the [[Leigh Leopards]] on [[Rugby league dual registration|DR]] loan of the 2022 season where he played twice and scored once.

===St Helens===
In 2021, it was reported that he went to [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in the Super League with a one-year deal. Norman would later in 2022 sign another contract for St Helens for another year. On 21 September 2023, it was announced that Norman would depart St Helens at the end of the 2023 Super League season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/st-helens-confirm-11-departures-including-overseas-star-will-hopoate/|title=St Helens confirm 11 departures, including overseas star Will Hopoate|website=www.loverugbyleague.com|date=22 September 2023 }}</ref>

===Leigh Leopards===
On 13 October 2023, it was reported that he had signed a permanent two-year deal to join Leigh Leopards for the 2024 season.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=13 Oct 2023|title=Leigh Leopards make first signing for 2024 as international forward returns on permanent deal: 'It doesn't feel like joining a new club'|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/leigh-leopards-make-first-signing-for-2024-as-international-forward-returns-on-permanent-deal-it-doesnt-feel-like-joining-a-new-club}}</ref>
Norman played 12 games for Leigh in the [[2024 Super League season]] which saw the club finish fifth on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/live-blog/12204/13227859/super-league-semi-finals-live-wigan-face-leigh-for-grand-final-spot/|title=Super League semi-finals: Wigan Warriors cruise past Leigh Leopards- live commentary and video clips|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>

On 22 November 2024 Dan announced his retirement from rugby league. As a result an early release was granted from his contract with the Leigh Leopards.

===Ireland===
In the 2022 [[2021 Rugby League World Cup|Rugby League World Cup]] he was announced in the [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] World Cup squad, and would later make his début versus [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] off of the bench.{{citation needed|date=August 2023}}
